title: Genetics, Reproduction, and Behavior Advanced Practice Test 1
description: Advanced mixed science practice with deeper genetics, behavior, and reproduction reasoning.

questions.prompt: In a Punnett square, what does each box represent?
questions.questionText: In a Punnett square, what does each box represent?
choices: One possible allele combination in an offspring
One trait the parents have already shown
A learned behavior the offspring might develop
The environment the offspring will grow up in
options: One possible allele combination in an offspring
One trait the parents have already shown
A learned behavior the offspring might develop
The environment the offspring will grow up in
questions.correctAnswer: One possible allele combination in an offspring
questions.explanation: Each box in a Punnett square shows one possible allele pairing the offspring could receive. The other options describe phenotype, behavior, or environment rather than the purpose of the boxes.

questions.prompt: Which pair correctly matches the term to its meaning?
questions.questionText: Which pair correctly matches the term to its meaning?
choices: Phenotype: observable trait
Genotype: learned behavior
Heterozygous: two identical alleles
Recessive: always shows when present
options: Phenotype: observable trait
Genotype: learned behavior
Heterozygous: two identical alleles
Recessive: always shows when present
questions.correctAnswer: Phenotype: observable trait
questions.explanation: Phenotype means the observable trait. Genotype is an allele combination, heterozygous means two different alleles, and a recessive trait does not always show when present.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows observational learning?
questions.questionText: Which example best shows observational learning?
choices: A young otter watches its parent crack shells with a rock and then copies the method
A turtle crawls toward water soon after hatching
A hydra grows a bud that breaks off
A plant spreads seeds by wind
options: A young otter watches its parent crack shells with a rock and then copies the method
A turtle crawls toward water soon after hatching
A hydra grows a bud that breaks off
A plant spreads seeds by wind
questions.correctAnswer: A young otter watches its parent crack shells with a rock and then copies the method
questions.explanation: Observational learning happens when an organism learns by watching another. The turtle shows instinct, the hydra shows budding, and the plant example is seed dispersal.

questions.prompt: Which option is an advantage of sexual reproduction?
questions.questionText: Which option is an advantage of sexual reproduction?
choices: It increases genetic variation in offspring
It always produces offspring faster than asexual reproduction
It creates identical offspring each time
It requires only one parent
options: It increases genetic variation in offspring
It always produces offspring faster than asexual reproduction
It creates identical offspring each time
It requires only one parent
questions.correctAnswer: It increases genetic variation in offspring
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction mixes genes from two parents, increasing variation. The other answers describe asexual reproduction or make false statements.

questions.prompt: Which type of asexual reproduction is correctly matched with its example?
questions.questionText: Which type of asexual reproduction is correctly matched with its example?
choices: Binary fission: one bacterium splits into two cells
Budding: a potato grows from a tuber
Vegetative reproduction: a hydra forms a side bud
Regeneration: a bird attracts a mate with a dance
options: Binary fission: one bacterium splits into two cells
Budding: a potato grows from a tuber
Vegetative reproduction: a hydra forms a side bud
Regeneration: a bird attracts a mate with a dance
questions.correctAnswer: Binary fission: one bacterium splits into two cells
questions.explanation: Binary fission is when one cell splits into two cells. Potatoes show vegetative reproduction, hydra show budding, and a mating dance is courtship behavior.

questions.prompt: Tall stems (T) are dominant to short stems (t). Two plants with genotype Tt are crossed. What percentage of offspring are expected to be short?
questions.questionText: Tall stems (T) are dominant to short stems (t). Two plants with genotype Tt are crossed. What percentage of offspring are expected to be short?
questions.explanation: A Tt × Tt cross gives TT, Tt, Tt, and tt. Only tt is short, so 1 out of 4 offspring, or 25%, are expected to be short. The other percentages do not match the Punnett square.

questions.prompt: In mice, black fur (B) is dominant to white fur (b). A black mouse and a white mouse have some white offspring. What must be true about the black mouse?
questions.questionText: In mice, black fur (B) is dominant to white fur (b). A black mouse and a white mouse have some white offspring. What must be true about the black mouse?
choices: It is heterozygous, Bb
It is homozygous dominant, BB
It is homozygous recessive, bb
Its genotype cannot be inferred at all
options: It is heterozygous, Bb
It is homozygous dominant, BB
It is homozygous recessive, bb
Its genotype cannot be inferred at all
questions.correctAnswer: It is heterozygous, Bb
questions.explanation: White offspring must be bb, so the black parent must contribute a recessive b allele. That means the black parent is Bb. BB could not produce white offspring with a white mouse.

questions.prompt: A plant with genotype Rr is crossed with a plant with genotype rr. Which statement is correct about the offspring?
questions.questionText: A plant with genotype Rr is crossed with a plant with genotype rr. Which statement is correct about the offspring?
choices: Half are expected to be Rr and half rr
All are expected to be Rr
Three-fourths are expected to be red
None can show the recessive trait
options: Half are expected to be Rr and half rr
All are expected to be Rr
Three-fourths are expected to be red
None can show the recessive trait
questions.correctAnswer: Half are expected to be Rr and half rr
questions.explanation: The Rr parent can pass R or r, while the rr parent can only pass r, giving Rr and rr in equal numbers. The other choices describe different crosses.

questions.prompt: A bird stops sounding an alarm call when a harmless lawn mower passes every day. Which behavior classification fits best?
questions.questionText: A bird stops sounding an alarm call when a harmless lawn mower passes every day. Which behavior classification fits best?
choices: habituation
conditioning
instinct
courtship
options: habituation
conditioning
instinct
courtship
questions.correctAnswer: habituation
questions.explanation: The bird is learning to ignore a repeated harmless stimulus, which is habituation. Conditioning links a signal to a result, instinct is inherited, and courtship attracts mates.

questions.prompt: Why is a strawberry patch started from runners likely to have plants that look very similar to one another?
questions.questionText: Why is a strawberry patch started from runners likely to have plants that look very similar to one another?
choices: Runners are a form of vegetative reproduction that usually makes genetically similar offspring
Runners are a type of courtship behavior that attracts similar mates
Runners create more genetic variation than seeds do
Runners change acquired traits into inherited traits
options: Runners are a form of vegetative reproduction that usually makes genetically similar offspring
Runners are a type of courtship behavior that attracts similar mates
Runners create more genetic variation than seeds do
Runners change acquired traits into inherited traits
questions.correctAnswer: Runners are a form of vegetative reproduction that usually makes genetically similar offspring
questions.explanation: Runners are vegetative reproduction, so the new plants are usually genetically similar to the parent. The other choices confuse reproduction with behavior or make incorrect claims about variation.

questions.prompt: A mother bear teaches cubs where to find salmon by leading them to the river and cubs copy her actions. Which combination best describes the cubs' learning?
questions.questionText: A mother bear teaches cubs where to find salmon by leading them to the river and cubs copy her actions. Which combination best describes the cubs' learning?
choices: observational learning
binary fission
instinct only
seed dispersal
options: observational learning
binary fission
instinct only
seed dispersal
questions.correctAnswer: observational learning
questions.explanation: The cubs are learning by watching and copying their mother, which is observational learning. The other choices refer to unrelated processes or ignore the evidence that the behavior was learned.

questions.prompt: A dandelion seed has a light body and parachute-like hairs. Which explanation best connects structure to function?
questions.questionText: A dandelion seed has a light body and parachute-like hairs. Which explanation best connects structure to function?
choices: The seed can be carried by wind to reduce crowding near the parent plant
The seed can attract mates for the flower
The seed will always grow into a genetically different plant
The hairs prove the seed was produced asexually
options: The seed can be carried by wind to reduce crowding near the parent plant
The seed can attract mates for the flower
The seed will always grow into a genetically different plant
The hairs prove the seed was produced asexually
questions.correctAnswer: The seed can be carried by wind to reduce crowding near the parent plant
questions.explanation: The light body and hairs help the seed travel on air currents, spreading offspring away from the parent. The other options confuse seed dispersal with courtship, genetics, or asexual reproduction.

questions.prompt: A red-flowered snapdragon with unknown genotype is crossed with a white-flowered snapdragon (rr). All 18 offspring are red. Which parent genotype is best supported by the results?
questions.questionText: A red-flowered snapdragon with unknown genotype is crossed with a white-flowered snapdragon (rr). All 18 offspring are red. Which parent genotype is best supported by the results?
choices: RR
Rr
rr
The red parent must be recessive
options: RR
Rr
rr
The red parent must be recessive
questions.correctAnswer: RR
questions.explanation: If the red parent were Rr, a cross with rr should produce some white offspring. Because all observed offspring are red, the best-supported genotype is RR. rr would not be red.

questions.prompt: Freckles (F) are dominant to no freckles (f). Two parents both have freckles, but one child has no freckles. Which pair of parent genotypes is possible?
questions.questionText: Freckles (F) are dominant to no freckles (f). Two parents both have freckles, but one child has no freckles. Which pair of parent genotypes is possible?
choices: Ff and Ff
FF and FF
FF and Ff only
ff and ff
options: Ff and Ff
FF and FF
FF and Ff only
ff and ff
questions.correctAnswer: Ff and Ff
questions.explanation: A child with no freckles must be ff, so each parent must contribute a recessive f allele. That means both parents can be Ff. Any parent with FF could not contribute f.

questions.prompt: A biologist crosses two smooth-seeded pea plants and gets 30 smooth offspring and 10 wrinkled offspring. Which explanation best fits the data?
questions.questionText: A biologist crosses two smooth-seeded pea plants and gets 30 smooth offspring and 10 wrinkled offspring. Which explanation best fits the data?
choices: Both smooth-seeded parents were probably heterozygous
Both smooth-seeded parents were definitely homozygous dominant
Wrinkled seeds must be the dominant trait
The results prove traits are acquired instead of inherited
options: Both smooth-seeded parents were probably heterozygous
Both smooth-seeded parents were definitely homozygous dominant
Wrinkled seeds must be the dominant trait
The results prove traits are acquired instead of inherited
questions.correctAnswer: Both smooth-seeded parents were probably heterozygous
questions.explanation: A roughly 3:1 smooth-to-wrinkled pattern fits a heterozygous × heterozygous cross when smooth is dominant. Homozygous dominant parents would not produce wrinkled offspring.

questions.prompt: Which statement best compares instinct and learned behavior using a real-life example?
questions.questionText: Which statement best compares instinct and learned behavior using a real-life example?
choices: A goose migrating is mostly instinctive, while a dog learning to respond to a whistle is learned
Both goose migration and whistle response are inherited instincts
Both are examples of vegetative reproduction
The whistle response is instinctive because it happens quickly
options: A goose migrating is mostly instinctive, while a dog learning to respond to a whistle is learned
Both goose migration and whistle response are inherited instincts
Both are examples of vegetative reproduction
The whistle response is instinctive because it happens quickly
questions.correctAnswer: A goose migrating is mostly instinctive, while a dog learning to respond to a whistle is learned
questions.explanation: Migration is an inherited behavior pattern, while whistle response is learned through experience or training. The other answers confuse instinct with learning or misuse reproduction terms.

questions.prompt: A certain fish species lays eggs and the male guards the nest until the eggs hatch. Which explanation best shows why this behavior matters?
questions.questionText: A certain fish species lays eggs and the male guards the nest until the eggs hatch. Which explanation best shows why this behavior matters?
choices: Guarding increases the chance that offspring survive to hatching
Guarding changes the genotype of the eggs
Guarding proves the eggs were made asexually
Guarding means the offspring will all be dominant
options: Guarding increases the chance that offspring survive to hatching
Guarding changes the genotype of the eggs
Guarding proves the eggs were made asexually
Guarding means the offspring will all be dominant
questions.correctAnswer: Guarding increases the chance that offspring survive to hatching
questions.explanation: Protective behavior helps more offspring survive by reducing danger from predators or disturbance. It does not change genetics or determine dominance.

questions.prompt: A farmer wants to predict whether crossing two horned cattle could ever produce a hornless calf. Hornless (h) is recessive. Which parent pair would make a hornless calf impossible?
questions.questionText: A farmer wants to predict whether crossing two horned cattle could ever produce a hornless calf. Hornless (h) is recessive. Which parent pair would make a hornless calf impossible?
choices: HH × Hh
Hh × Hh
Hh × hh
HH × hh
options: HH × Hh
Hh × Hh
Hh × hh
HH × hh
questions.correctAnswer: HH × Hh
questions.explanation: A hornless calf would need genotype hh. In an HH × Hh cross, at least one H always appears, so hh is impossible. The other crosses can produce hh or include a recessive parent.

questions.prompt: A student says, 'If two parents show the dominant trait, all their children will too.' Which example best proves this statement is not always true?
questions.questionText: A student says, 'If two parents show the dominant trait, all their children will too.' Which example best proves this statement is not always true?
choices: Two heterozygous parents can have an offspring with the recessive phenotype
A homozygous dominant parent always passes on a dominant allele
Some acquired traits appear after birth
Habitual behaviors can change over time
options: Two heterozygous parents can have an offspring with the recessive phenotype
A homozygous dominant parent always passes on a dominant allele
Some acquired traits appear after birth
Habitual behaviors can change over time
questions.correctAnswer: Two heterozygous parents can have an offspring with the recessive phenotype
questions.explanation: If both parents are heterozygous, a recessive offspring is still possible. That directly shows dominant-looking parents do not guarantee all dominant-looking children.

questions.prompt: Which comparison best explains why seed dispersal and parental care are both survival strategies?
questions.questionText: Which comparison best explains why seed dispersal and parental care are both survival strategies?
choices: Both improve offspring survival, but one moves offspring to safer places while the other directly protects them
Both are learned behaviors used only by animals
Both happen only during asexual reproduction
Both guarantee every offspring lives to adulthood
options: Both improve offspring survival, but one moves offspring to safer places while the other directly protects them
Both are learned behaviors used only by animals
Both happen only during asexual reproduction
Both guarantee every offspring lives to adulthood
questions.correctAnswer: Both improve offspring survival, but one moves offspring to safer places while the other directly protects them
questions.explanation: Seed dispersal can reduce crowding and competition, while parental care can defend or support young directly. The other choices incorrectly limit where these strategies occur or overstate their results.

questions.prompt: A red flower plant crossed with a white flower plant produces 50% red and 50% white offspring. If red is dominant, which genotype must the red parent have?
questions.questionText: A red flower plant crossed with a white flower plant produces 50% red and 50% white offspring. If red is dominant, which genotype must the red parent have?
choices: Rr
RR
rr
The genotype cannot be narrowed down
options: Rr
RR
rr
The genotype cannot be narrowed down
questions.correctAnswer: Rr
questions.explanation: A 1:1 red-to-white outcome from a cross with a white plant (rr) fits Rr × rr. RR × rr would give all red offspring, so the red parent must be heterozygous.

questions.prompt: A puppy first ignores the vacuum after hearing it safely many times, then later learns to sit when it hears a clicker for treats. Which sequence correctly names the two learned behaviors?
questions.questionText: A puppy first ignores the vacuum after hearing it safely many times, then later learns to sit when it hears a clicker for treats. Which sequence correctly names the two learned behaviors?
choices: habituation, then conditioning
conditioning, then habituation
instinct, then courtship
playing, then vegetative reproduction
options: habituation, then conditioning
conditioning, then habituation
instinct, then courtship
playing, then vegetative reproduction
questions.correctAnswer: habituation, then conditioning
questions.explanation: Ignoring a repeated harmless vacuum is habituation. Learning that a clicker signal leads to treats is conditioning. The other sequences reverse or misuse the terms.

questions.prompt: A plant breeder wants more genetic variation to help develop disease-resistant crops. Which strategy best fits that goal?
questions.questionText: A plant breeder wants more genetic variation to help develop disease-resistant crops. Which strategy best fits that goal?
choices: Use sexual reproduction by crossing different parent plants
Use only runners from one parent plant
Use only tubers from one parent plant
Use only cuttings from one parent plant
options: Use sexual reproduction by crossing different parent plants
Use only runners from one parent plant
Use only tubers from one parent plant
Use only cuttings from one parent plant
questions.correctAnswer: Use sexual reproduction by crossing different parent plants
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction mixes genes from two parents, increasing variation. Runners, tubers, and cuttings are asexual methods that usually keep offspring more genetically similar.

questions.prompt: Why is the statement 'all traits are either inherited or acquired, never both influenced' too simple?
questions.questionText: Why is the statement 'all traits are either inherited or acquired, never both influenced' too simple?
choices: Some traits are inherited, but the environment can still affect how strongly they appear
All traits are completely learned after birth
Inherited traits never depend on genes
Acquired traits are always passed directly to offspring
options: Some traits are inherited, but the environment can still affect how strongly they appear
All traits are completely learned after birth
Inherited traits never depend on genes
Acquired traits are always passed directly to offspring
questions.correctAnswer: Some traits are inherited, but the environment can still affect how strongly they appear
questions.explanation: Genes set up many traits, but environment can affect expression, size, or health. The other choices wrongly deny genetics or claim acquired traits are inherited.

questions.prompt: Two tall plants produce only tall offspring in repeated crosses. Which conclusion is best supported, though not absolutely guaranteed forever?
questions.questionText: Two tall plants produce only tall offspring in repeated crosses. Which conclusion is best supported, though not absolutely guaranteed forever?
choices: At least one parent is likely homozygous dominant for height
Both parents must be heterozygous
Height must be a recessive trait
The offspring have no genotype
options: At least one parent is likely homozygous dominant for height
Both parents must be heterozygous
Height must be a recessive trait
The offspring have no genotype
questions.correctAnswer: At least one parent is likely homozygous dominant for height
questions.explanation: Repeatedly seeing only tall offspring suggests the cross may include a homozygous dominant parent, making recessive outcomes unlikely or impossible. Two heterozygous parents should eventually produce some short offspring.
